comment.body: Hey Craig, why don’t you offer your view of today’s post then we can critique our understandings. — My take on today’s beautiful analysis. No intraday edge unless you are premium selling. The weekly post is still very important so taking that into mind. Mean reversion longs are still on the cards Maybe you could look to go long with a tight stop at the support around 6015 or even at 6072 ☠️  but you want to see vol getting eaten there before you go long if we get that low.

comment.body:
Let’s discuss, it is the only way to better understand the dynamic !!!! This is mine, correct me if I am wrong, would appreciate that !
As time passes trough the session, that 6275 is getting bigger, MM sold premium there, and they will sell more as time passes. Furthermore we have that positive speed environment 6100-6145 that eats vol as spot goes up and gex exp increase = the tangent line of the curve become steeper to the upside, so MM will sell again the price. 
But what if spot moves down? That environment will favour for acceleration in fact as spot moves down we have less gex there and the speed curve is steeper to the downside. Then we have that 6107-6115 which become supportive as gex increases there as spot moves down.

comment.body:
Some people are still thinking in two dimensions...
Alma didn’t say bullish moon rocket emoji, etc. She said the market is supported until February OPEX. And SPX was at 5969 when she said that. But she also mentioned in chat the other day that things don’t look great after that—she’ll write more about it later.
She also pointed out today that these vol-suppressive long skew positions are expiring today and tomorrow, meaning they’ll disappear from the market.
She literally predicted this Thursday volatility in her Sunday post. So… yeah. And that’s probably why she tightened her stop-losses... maybe not yesterday, but recently... and bought OTM puts, because she expected this
